signals.headline: Trump Announces Two-Week Ceasefire with Iran Ahead of 8 p
signals.raw:
Trump Announces Two-Week Ceasefire with Iran Ahead of 8 p.m. Deadline | YES | HIGH confidence**  
President Donald Trump announced Tuesday evening that the U.S. reached a two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran, just before an 8 p.m. ET deadline, amid threats over reopening the Strait of Hormuz.[1]  
This follows earlier collapsed talks and a U.S. Navy blockade announcement after failed negotiation

signals.raw:
**Democratic Party** recruitment win: Former Sen. **Sherrod Brown** announced he will challenge Husted—no date specified in results, but framed as a major development amid GOP optimism for holding/expanding their 53-47 majority despite midterm headwinds.[2]

signals.headline: **JD Vance** vacated his Ohio Senate seat after becoming vice president; **Jon Husted** (R) was appointed to fill it and
signals.raw: **JD Vance** vacated his Ohio Senate seat after becoming vice president; **Jon Husted** (R) was appointed to fill it and is running in 2026 for the remaining term.[2]

signals.raw: Price holds ~$1.36 amid sideways trading and regulatory uncertainty; analysts split on April targets ($1.15 bearish to $1.60-$7.52 bullish), pending CLARITY Act Senate markup (post-April 13 return, no update) and FOMC (April 28-29)[1][2][3][5].
